questions.question: Why is average throughput a weak guide for queueing risk?
options: Because short periods where arrivals exceed service capacity create backlog even if the daily average looks safe.
Because queues only matter for storage systems.
Because average throughput is always higher than peak throughput.
Because service rate cannot be measured directly.
questions.explanation: Burst windows, not steady-state averages, drive queue build-up.

questions.question: What is the first quantity to compute during a burst?
options: The number of regions in the fleet
questions.explanation: Backlog growth starts with how much demand exceeds processing.

questions.question: If arrivals are 60K/s, service is 40K/s, and the burst lasts 10 minutes, what backlog forms?
questions.explanation: Backlog growth is 20K/s for 600 seconds, which is 12M items.

questions.question: What additional number is required to estimate recovery time after a burst?
options: The number of engineers on call
The total number of API routes
The size of the primary database
questions.explanation: Drain time depends on how much extra capacity is available once the burst ends.

questions.question: Which hidden factor often turns an expected burst into a much worse incident?
options: Client retries that amplify effective arrival rate
questions.explanation: Retries can create multiplicative overload during incidents.

questions.question: Which metric best validates whether queueing assumptions are failing in production?
questions.explanation: Depth and age directly show backlog health.

questions.question: What trade-off comes with buffering instead of shedding?
options: Better short-term acceptance, but higher tail latency and possible stale work.
Lower latency with no extra memory use.
questions.explanation: Buffers absorb spikes by accepting delayed completion.

questions.question: When is dropping work often better than queueing it?
options: When the work loses value quickly and long backlog age would make it useless.
When the service has exactly one region.
When the interviewer asks for a durable system.
questions.explanation: Some work is more valuable to reject early than to complete too late.
